cited_evidence.quote: In terms of the aftermarket, the trading activities, that inflection point, we need a couple of things to occur. We are seeing continued utilization of green time. cited_evidence.reasoning: The delayed inflection in parts trading—the highest-margin recovery segment—undermines near-term demand recovery expectations. Pilot shortages keeping aircraft grounded directly suppresses parts availability and demand signals.

cited_evidence.quote: Global USMs that are still about 30% below 2019 levels. This is most relevant for our parts activities, which are our most international. cited_evidence.quote: Our operating margin was 6.9% on an adjusted basis, up from 5.5% last year. We continue to demonstrate that the structural changes we made during the pandemic have resulted in sustained margin improvement.

cited_evidence.quote: Our customers, as you've heard from them, they're seeing the exact same labor pressures we are. attribution.model_notes: live attribution: The -7.1% move is a blend of structural and transient forces. Structural headwinds: (1) management credibility erosion—repeated delays in inflection guidance and FY23 guidance withdrawal signal lost visibility and near-term momentum loss; (2) demand deceleration—commercial sales growth modest (10% YoY but flat sequentially), and the critical parts recovery inflection pushed from H1 to H2 due to pilot shortages and green-time utilization. Macro triggers: (3) FOMC 75bps hike + hawkish SEP on 2022-09-21 + UK gilt crisis on 2022-09-23 created a sudden risk-off event that dampens aerospace sector sentiment and recovery confidence. Offsetting tailwinds: (1) pricing power—67bps margin expansion YoY and successful labor pass-throughs show operational resilience; (2) competitive wins in distribution and government contracts (USAFE, Textron, Norwegian Air) support longer-term moat. The move appears justified by a combination of macro shock (30% weight) + credibility miss (23% weight) + demand softness (15% weight), only partially offset by pricing strength (12%) and competitive gains (20%).
